title: Promote all runnable examples into explicit self-host parity targets
goal: Ensure every example that the Go implementation can run has an explicit self-host parity classification and a path into the bootstrap gate.
context: Start from the gap inventory created by SELFHOST-001.
Use examples/ and tests/examples_test.go to discover the runnable example set.
Some examples may require staged support; classify rather than hiding them.
steps: Build or update a self-host parity manifest in scripts or tests that lists examples as supported, expected-failing with reason, or out-of-scope for now.
Add test coverage that fails if a new example lacks a self-host parity classification.
Promote already-supported examples into scripts/selfhost_bootstrap_check.sh or the appropriate stage check.
Update SELFHOST_WORK.md with the next unsupported example or feature dependency.
done_when: All examples have an explicit self-host parity status.
The bootstrap gate covers all examples currently marked supported.
No supported self-host example is only documented informally.
